title: MemorySubstrateAuditCompleted payload
description: Emitted by the chain memory-substrate-within-vault T7 retrospective to record that the vault-mediated memory substrate (memory forge schema + dispatch + MemoryWritten event + SessionStart materialization hook + one-shot harness migration + parse_context memory-aware resolution + arc-close-hook forge routing) has been verified against the chain's completion criteria. Findings are keyed to docs/MEMORY_SUBSTRATE.md sections and the chain's completion-condition items (a)-(h). Self-hosting check: the substrate's own write-side events ledger records the closing audit.

audit_doc.description: Pointer to the audit source-doc the findings were keyed against — typically docs/MEMORY_SUBSTRATE.md (T1 design) + the retrospective doc at docs/MEMORY_SUBSTRATE_RETROSPECTIVE_<date>.md.

summary.description: One- or two-paragraph executive summary of the audit outcome — what landed, what was deferred, which downstream chains become forgeable now that the substrate has shipped.

recommended_next_phase.description: Free-text recommendation for the next phase of work (e.g. 'Run chain 299 to disable the legacy harness auto-memory writer and reconcile the orphaned direct-writes'). Optional.

findings.description: One entry per audit checklist item. Severity uses the audit-convention enum (low / medium / high / critical); use n/a for informational findings.

item.description: Audit checklist item identifier and short description.

status.description: pass = criterion satisfied; fail = criterion not satisfied (must file follow-on); partial = satisfied with documented gap; deferred = explicitly out of scope for this chain.

evidence.description: Concrete evidence supporting status — SQL query result, test name, commit SHA, doc pointer, etc.

severity.description: Severity if status is fail/partial. n/a for informational rows.
